description.en:
New Zealand's charity regulator, Charities Services, assigns each registered charity a Charities Registration Number. This is often referred to as a 'CC' number because it typically begins with those letters.

Only registered charities receive a Charities Registration Number. Registration under the Charities Act 2005 is voluntary, although registration is generally required to access charitable tax exemptions and to describe the organisation as a registered charitable entity. [1]

[1] https://www.charities.govt.nz/charities-in-new-zealand/the-charities-register/about-the-charities-register

access.onlineAccessDetails:
There is a basic and advanced search available via the Charities Service website. It is possible to search for charities by name, registration number, New Zealand Business Number, street address, or officer name.

This how-to guide explains more: https://www.charities.govt.nz/charities-in-new-zealand/the-charities-register/charities-data-how-to-guide
access.guidanceOnLocatingIds: Registration numbers consist of the letters 'CC' followed by a 5-digit number.

data.dataAccessDetails: See the website's Open Data information: https://www.charities.govt.nz/charities-in-new-zealand/the-charities-register/open-data
